title: rhinanthus angustifolius subsp. grandiflorus
intro: Rhinanthus angustifolius subsp. grandiflorus, commonly known as the large-flowered rampion, is a subspecies of flowering plant in the family Orobanchaceae. This parasitic plant obtains its nutrients by attaching itself to the roots of other plants, often grasses and wildflowers. Traditionally, Rhinanthus angustifolius subsp. grandiflorus has been used in herbal medicine for its diuretic and expectorant properties, which can help to relieve symptoms of respiratory issues such as bronchitis and coughs. Herbal preparations of the plant include infusions and decoctions, often used to treat respiratory ailments and promote overall well-being.
